needs.explanation_en: Correct. Adjusting weights in the direction of the gradient computed through backpropagation reduces errors and improves accuracy.
This is a rule-based system. Deep learning is data-driven automatic learning.
This is the k-NN algorithm, which differs from neural network weight learning.
Deep learning requires iterative weight adjustments to improve accuracy. Fixed weights cannot lead to improvement.
This is a security and storage concept unrelated to the model's text processing mechanism.
Correct. A token is a unit of division, and embedding is its numerical representation.
This describes dataset identification and transformation methods, not the actual definition of a token.
This is an implementation detail that does not explain the conceptual role of tokens and embeddings.
Textract is a document image processing service and CloudWatch is a general monitoring tool, neither of which provides model bias detection or explainability.
Rekognition is an image recognition service and CloudTrail is an audit logging service, neither of which meets the core requirements for responsible AI.
Correct. Clarify provides bias detection and explainability, while Model Monitor offers data drift detection and performance monitoring directly.
Comprehend is a natural language processing service and GuardDuty is a security threat detection service, neither of which provides model bias detection or drift monitoring capabilities.
Correct. Overfitting and underfitting are distinguished by the gap between training and validation performance.
The number of parameters and data volume have an impact, but alone they cannot accurately distinguish between the two phenomena.
Overfitting and underfitting can occur in all problems regardless of model type.
Regularization is one approach to solving overfitting but not the only one. Underfitting can be addressed by increasing model complexity and other methods.
Invoking an expensive model for every report would incur excessive costs when processing large volumes.
Correct. Embedding-based search processes large volumes of reports at low cost while maintaining high accuracy.
Weekly retraining is excessive in cost and time, and deploying a model every week is unsuitable for real-time processing.
Using images solely for validation purposes cannot provide accurate responses when text and image information conflict.
